Off Calendar Oct-19-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JESS BOOTH, ESQ. AND RAY WONG, ESQ., DUANE MORRIS, 415.957.3116 AND 415.957.3149, FOR PLAINTIFF JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL.
GABRIEL NEIL RUBIN, ESQ. AND ELIZABETH WILLIAMS, ESQ., KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK & GONZO, 415.926.7600, FOR DEFENDANT COURNALE & CO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: Defendants Cournale & Co. (Cal Dre License No. 00322534), Clyde Cournale, Curtis Cournale, William Schmetterer's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Supplemental Designation Of Expert Witnesses. The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Judge Pro Tem Halperin as follows: Denied. Although Plaintiff's narrative concerning Mr. Blanchard's testimony is not a model of specificity, it gives sufficient notice of what Mr. Blanchard will say at trial to allow Defendants to assess whether to take Mr. Blanchard's deposition, to fully explore the relevant subject area at any such deposition, and "gear up to cross-examine" Mr. Blanchard effectively. Nor is Mr. Blanchard's designation impermissibly duplicative of the anticipated subjects of Plaintiff's previously-designated expert Mr. Littlefield. (counsel to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: AARON SEDON, ESQ., KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK & GONZO, 415.402.0059, FOR DEFENDANTS COURNALE & CO., CLYDE COURNALE, CURTIS COURNALE AND WILLiaM SCHMETTERER; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS COURNALE & CO. (CAL DRE LICENSE NO. 00322534), CLYDE COURNALE, CURTIS COURNALE, WILLIAM SCHMETTERER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Denied as to Defendant Clyde Cournale and granted with fifteen days leave to amend as to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action. There are sufficient allegations as to Defendant Clyde Cournale. Plaintiff is given leave to amend the first cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that all of the holders of notes agreed in writing to be bound by the procedure outlined in section 2941.9 and that such agreement was recorded or that recording was not made and should not be required. Plaintiff is given leave to amend the second cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a basis for seeking attorney's fees and ultimate facts sufficient to support the claim. Plaintiff is given leave to amend the third and fourth causes of action to amend to plead whether the alleged contract is written, oral or implied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, not reported.
